title: Mutation and aliases deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Mutation and aliases; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Mutation and aliases: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Assigning or updating through one name can never affect a value observed through another name.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. First trace which names reach the same mutable object, then distinguish changing that object from rebinding one name to a different value.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. First trace which names reach the same mutable object, then distinguish changing that object from rebinding one name to a different value.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Mutation and aliases instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: First trace which names reach the same mutable object, then distinguish changing that object from rebinding one name to a different value.
Assigning or updating through one name can never affect a value observed through another name.

answer.explanation: First trace which names reach the same mutable object, then distinguish changing that object from rebinding one name to a different value.
